_schema: closing_hour_history v1 — daily Closing Hour Pulse readings (S&P 500 institutional closing-hour flow, 3–4pm ET, volume-weighted, scored −100…+100). One snapshot per Bot post. 'date' = session covered; 'ts' = post time (UTC). 'score' = headline flow score (−100 max selling … +100 max buying); 'flow' = SELLING/BUYING/NEUTRAL label; 'price_state' = the feed's price read (e.g. sideways/up/down); 'spy10d' = SPY % over the trailing 10 sessions; 'closes' = last-5 daily closing-hour directions, newest LAST ('green' up / 'red' down); 'divergence' = the one-line flow-vs-price call; 'narrative' = the post's paragraph verbatim. NOTE: the feed's 90-session cumulative chart + SPY overlay + bias subchart are an IMAGE and are NOT transcribed — this store builds its own forward daily-score history from here.
_rules: Only confirmed feed numbers. Append-only — never edit or delete a committed snapshot. Never invent or carry-forward; if a field wasn't shown, omit it.

history.divergence: Institutions selling the close into a sideways tape — hidden distribution, risk building.
history.narrative: Institutional flow turned sharply negative into the close at −18, with institutions distributing into a market that's made essentially no progress over ten days. This divergence between selling pressure and sideways price action is textbook distribution — institutions moving size without moving the market, which typically precedes either a breakdown or a flush lower before any sustained move higher. Watch support levels on any gap down tomorrow; accumulation doesn't follow this pattern.

history.divergence: Flow and price aligned — close is confirming.
history.narrative: Institutional flow turned balanced into the close at -9/100, showing no meaningful directional conviction from large players in the final hour. SPY's sideways 10-day performance at -0.5% aligns with this lack of momentum, confirming institutions aren't pushing aggressively in either direction. Expect consolidation to continue until we see flow shift materially away from neutral.

history.divergence: Flow and price aligned — close is confirming.
history.narrative: Institutional selling pressure intensified into the close with a flow score of -19, marking notably negative positioning in the final hour. This alignment with SPY's 10-day decline of -1.2% confirms the downtrend is intact and institutions remain committed to reducing exposure rather than defending levels. The consistency between flow and price suggests this weakness has conviction behind it.

history.divergence: Flow and price aligned — close is confirming.
history.narrative: Institutional flow turned balanced in the closing hour with a -10 score, which aligns with SPY's essentially flat 10-day performance. The agreement between neutral flow and sideways price action suggests the market is genuinely range-bound rather than being held up or down by directional conviction. Watch for conviction to return from either direction to confirm a breakout from this consolidation.

history.divergence: Flow and price aligned — close is confirming.
history.narrative: Institutional flow turned balanced at close with a -16 score, showing neither conviction buying nor selling into the final hour. This alignment with SPY's 1.1% loss over ten days suggests the market is consolidating rather than attracting fresh institutional demand on weakness, which typically precedes either capitulation selling or stabilization. Without aggressive institutional accumulation at these levels, the path of least resistance remains down until we see flow turn positive on a price dip.
